title: release/18.x:  [InstCombine] Fix unexpected overwriting in `foldSelectWithSRem` (#89539)
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by tightening the pattern match to ensure the same remainder operand is used in both the `srem` and `add` operations. The original pattern matched any `add` with an `srem` operand, which was too broad and could miscompile. The fix uses `m_Specific(RemRes)` to bind the `srem` result directly to the first `add` operand, guaranteeing the transformation only fires when `%add = add i32 %srem, %n` and `%srem = srem i32 %x, %n` share the same divisor `%n`.

This ensures correctness while preserving the core optimization: converting `select (icmp slt rem, 0), add(rem, n), rem` into non-negative remainder (Euclidean division) when `n` is a power of two. The generalized test cases verify the pattern works for arbitrary power-of-two divisors and constant folding scenarios.

**Key takeaway**: When generalizing from a specific test, enforce operand identity constraints to prevent invalid transformations, and verify with varied test cases that cover the intended semantic pattern.
